responses.global_analysis: 1. JSON Match: Hardcoded lyrics and reference JSON provided a solid spelling baseline, but required extensive manual transcription for Jibun ROCK's middle verse and outro as they were missing from the reference JSON. 2. JSON Usage: Fully utilized the reference for Song 2 (first verse) and Song 3, while switching to manual transcription for the rest. 3. Timing & Pacing: Subtitles are precisely aligned to the vocal onset with logical line breaks to ensure maximum readability during fast-paced segments. Included prominent on-screen song title cards as silent visual events.

subtitles.original: カルマ
subtitles.english: [Title: Karma]

subtitles.original: じぶんROCK
subtitles.english: [Title: Jibun ROCK]

subtitles.original: 今日も君は
subtitles.english: Today, you also...

subtitles.original: 信じること 忘れずに目覚められているか？
subtitles.english: ...are you awake without forgetting to believe?

subtitles.original: 時が経つと
subtitles.english: As time passes...

subtitles.original: 自分さえも 信じられなくなる時代なのです
subtitles.english: ...is it an era where you can't even believe in yourself?

subtitles.original: 自分では気付かないの
subtitles.english: I don't realize it myself,

subtitles.original: がこの症状の特徴で
subtitles.english: and that's the characteristic of this symptom.

subtitles.original: 唯一ある予防法は
subtitles.english: The only way to prevent it...

subtitles.original: 自分に嘘だきゃつかないこと
subtitles.english: ...is to not tell lies to yourself.

subtitles.original: 忘れてたまるもんかよ！！
subtitles.english: Don't you ever forget it!!

subtitles.original: 僕がボクじゃなくなったら
subtitles.english: If I'm not me anymore,

subtitles.original: たとえ生きてたって
subtitles.english: even if I lived,

subtitles.original: 生きてる意味すら見つからんだろ
subtitles.english: I wouldn't find the meaning of living.

subtitles.original: 声にならない叫びは
subtitles.english: The scream that can't become a voice,

subtitles.original: 僕が声を枯らして
subtitles.english: I'll lose my voice...

subtitles.original: 叫んで歌って
subtitles.english: ...shouting and singing,

subtitles.original: 伝わるまでわめいて
subtitles.english: crying out until it reaches you,

subtitles.original: 届くまで止めない！！
subtitles.english: I won't stop until it reaches you!

subtitles.original: 今日も地球は回り続ける
subtitles.english: Today, the earth continues to spin,

subtitles.original: 誰が泣いても怒っても止まらず
subtitles.english: never stopping no matter who cries or gets angry.

subtitles.original: 試しに僕が風邪をひいても
subtitles.english: Even if I were to catch a cold as a test,

subtitles.original: もちろん止まることなんてねえわ
subtitles.english: of course it would never stop.

subtitles.original: 人生も同じように
subtitles.english: Life is the exact same way,

subtitles.original: 止まることはなく進んでいくから
subtitles.english: it keeps moving forward without stopping.

subtitles.original: だからそうさ今日も人は
subtitles.english: So that's why today, people once again

subtitles.original: お手手のシワとシワをあわせんの
subtitles.english: press their hands together in prayer.

subtitles.original: 終わらせちまう事なんか
subtitles.english: Ending things...

subtitles.original: 誰にだってできんだろ？
subtitles.english: ...is something anyone can do, right?

subtitles.original: たとえ終わっちまったって
subtitles.english: Even if it comes to an end,

subtitles.original: 次のスタート すぐ切れんだろ？！
subtitles.english: you can start anew right away, can't you?!

subtitles.original: 今君が思うキセキも
subtitles.english: Even the miracle you're thinking of right now,

subtitles.original: 底上げさせてしまえば
subtitles.english: if you raise its baseline,

subtitles.original: もうその時点でそのキセキは
subtitles.english: at that very moment, that miracle...

subtitles.original: 君にとっちゃ必然のお仲間さ
subtitles.english: ...becomes an inevitable companion to you.

subtitles.original: WOW!!
subtitles.english: WOW!!

subtitles.original: 何回ダメダメと言われようが
subtitles.english: No matter how many times they say 'no, no',

subtitles.original: 実際問題そんなん関係なくて
subtitles.english: practically speaking, that doesn't matter.

subtitles.original: 今自分自身
subtitles.english: Right now, within yourself,

subtitles.original: 必要なものたくわえてそろえとけよ
subtitles.english: gather and prepare what you need!

subtitles.original: なぁそうだろ？！
subtitles.english: Hey, isn't that right?!

subtitles.original: まだまだ時間はまだあるぞ
subtitles.english: There's still, still time left.

subtitles.original: 先見の明 今は研ぎ澄まして
subtitles.english: Hone your foresight right now,

subtitles.original: 主人公は一人だけ
subtitles.english: There's only one main character.

subtitles.original: 自分の物語のはじまり
subtitles.english: It's the start of your own story!

subtitles.original: 星座になれたら
subtitles.english: [Title: If I Could Become a Constellation]

subtitles.original: もうすぐ時計は6時
subtitles.english: The clock is almost 6

subtitles.original: もうそこに一番星
subtitles.english: There's already the first star

subtitles.original: 影を踏んで 夜に紛れたくなる帰り道
subtitles.english: Stepping on shadows, wanting to blend into the night on the way home

subtitles.original: どんなに探してみても
subtitles.english: No matter how much I search

subtitles.original: 一つしかない星
subtitles.english: There's only one star

responses.thoughts:
**Finalized Subtitle Generation Plan**

Okay, here's my plan. I've been given a video segment and a set of rules for generating audio-synchronized subtitles. The core objective is to create accurate, contextually relevant subtitles, primarily based on vocal audio (my ground truth), but with visual elements subtitled as well. Here's how I'm going to approach this:

I'm starting by carefully analyzing the provided information: The goal is to create audio-synchronized subtitles for the video, prioritizing vocal audio as the ground truth. There are several rules to follow: Using vocal events as the absolute guide, I'll transcribe/subtitle on-screen text as well. I'll adhere to a decoder hierarchy (lyrics, context, JSON reference), respecting timecode constraints. Subtitles should be precisely aligned, truncated when needed, and in the MM:SS.mmm format, capped at 50 characters per line, and no closed caption tags, only JSON output.

I have reference JSON data for three songs: "Karma", "Jibun ROCK", and "Seiza ni Naretara". I'm looking for vocalizations. For "Karma", I'll check if the vocalizations "Woah..." should be subtitled, which I'll omit unless the wordless vocalizations are a part of substantial lyrics, or if there is any visual text, e.g. a title card at the start.

I'll break the video into segments:

*   **Segment 1: "Karma" (00:00 - 00:25):** The goal is to capture any "Karma" title card and disregard any non-lyrical humming.
    *   I'll start by checking the audio and any visual elements at the start of "Karma." I'll then transcribe the title card "カルマ" (Karma) if it is present.
    *   "Karma" includes vocals. I'll not add lyrics based on the reference JSON because the video does not sing lyrics from the reference JSON.
*   **Segment 2: "Jibun ROCK" (00:26 - 04:12):** This requires careful manual transcription and translation, especially since the reference JSON is incomplete.
    *   I'll start with the "じぶんROCK" title card and proceed by meticulously transcribing the vocal sections of "Jibun ROCK." I'll make sure to match the transcription to the audio, using the reference data where it exists, and performing manual transcription (and translation) where it is incomplete or absent.
    *   My focus will be on timing, accuracy, and line breaks to fit the 50-character limit. I'll listen to the audio repeatedly and transcribe accurately. I will check for any burnt-in text on the screen, verifying spelling, and using these as tie-breakers. I also have to translate any new lyrics.
*   **Segment 3: "Seiza ni Naretara" (04:13 - 05:01):**
    *   Here I'll identify and capture title cards, and transcribe lyrics, accurately aligning with the audio.

I'll refine the timecodes by listening to the audio closely and adjusting the timestamps in the JSON output, including logical line breaks based on the context and timing. For "Jibun ROCK," I must transcribe its middle and outro sections which is not present in the reference. I must make sure that all timestamps are in the MM:SS.mmm format, complete with zero-padding. I'll be certain to include a detailed `global_analysis` within the JSON output, which explains how I handled the different parts of the video.

The JSON output will be structured as follows: First, I will have a `global_analysis` section to document the decision-making process. Then, there'll be a `subs` array containing individual subtitle entries, each with `start`, `end`, `og` (original text), and `en` (English translation) fields. The subtitle translations will be very straightforward, and will not add any unnecessary wording. I will verify that all translations are accurate. I will ensure the 50-character line limit is followed. I will use the format MM:SS.mmm for the timestamps. Visual title cards (like "[Title: Jibun ROCK]") are allowed, but no CC tags.

I will then check that everything is in JSON format, and deliver the final result, confident that the subtitles are accurate, precisely timed, and adhere to all given constraints!
